objective: Freeze strict proposal/proof contracts and replay one bounded structural game-form target while recording the Stiller/Elkies orthodox-chess boundary as unsupported.
non_negotiables: Candidate proposals must be frozen and hashed before any verifier call.
A future ranker may receive only target.ranker_view, proposal.position, and proposal.proposal_features.
The executable fallback target is a bounded structural game form, not an exact CGT value modulo arbitrary equivalence.
The bounded fallback uses board-syntax-only legality and must not be promoted as an orthodox-chess position.
The Stiller/Elkies positions are legal historical anchors but remain unsupported exact targets under the current solver domain.
Target-aware discovery authority is partizan.verifier_result.v0.1; legacy partizan.dataset_label.v0 rows are not silently inferred or bound.
All new evidence records immutable forty-character commits; code_commit=workspace is forbidden.
Wave 68 makes no learned-search, agency, temperature, aesthetic-performance, or nontrivial orthodox-chess CGT claim.

tasks.prompt: Implement five strict v0.1 discovery contracts, typed identities, canonical bytes, cross-object validation, and proposal-only ranker-input extraction. Use bounded_structural_game_form and board_syntax_only for the fallback target.
acceptance: All five schemas reject additional properties.
The valid bundle has one certified target, one certified other form, one rejection, and one error.
The verifier-leak proposal fails validation.
Ranker-input bytes contain no verifier, label, certificate, result, rejection, expanded-node, or ordinal fields.
Raw JSON floats, dirty commits, and code_commit=workspace fail.
Contract names do not imply arbitrary CGT equivalence or orthodox-chess legality.

tasks.prompt: Record the Stiller kernel and initial Elkies composition, reproduce their current domain rejection, and distinguish the legal historical anchor from the bounded board-form fallback.
acceptance: The report distinguishes historical inspiration from replayed evidence.
The report states that the historical FENs parse legally but fail the current exact-domain gate.
The fallback is named bounded_structural_game_form with board_syntax_only legality.
No unsupported solver capability is inferred.

tasks.prompt: Expose a deterministic batch target verifier by refactoring the current bounded-board replay path. Distinguish verified_match, verified_nonmatch, rejected, and internal_error, bind the declared rule and node budget, and compare Thermograph domain-separated SHA-256 structural identities.
outputs: Nonmatch, rejection, corruption, and node-budget controls
acceptance: Wave 55 row 090 matches the SHA-256 structural target under the declared bounded rule.
A wrong target is verified_nonmatch rather than a verifier error.
The Stiller kernel remains a structured rejection under the current domain.
Unsupported rules, stale evidence, and exhausted node budgets fail deterministically.
Legacy 64-bit digests are not accepted as target identities.
Output order is deterministic.

tasks.prompt: Add candidate-pool, verification, and replay-run orchestration. Freeze and hash the complete pool before verification, compare proposal generation twice, capture all source commits, and emit a validated input-order replay bundle.
acceptance: A byte mismatch prevents pool publication.
The candidate pool is complete, validated, and hashed before verification begins.
The manifest binds target, proposals, generator configuration, seed, and four immutable commits.
The replay log stays within budget and all summary fields recompute from calls and results.

tasks.prompt: Generate and audit the four-candidate Wave 68 bundle. Verify proposal-only ranker inputs and preserve the explicit block on learned-search and nontrivial orthodox-chess CGT claims.
outputs: Checked-in bounded target and four-candidate discovery bundle
acceptance: The proposal artifact contains four unique candidates and is byte-identical across two generations.
Exactly four verifier results and four budgeted calls are recorded.
At least one candidate matches the bounded target and at least one is rejected.
All new evidence records immutable Partizan, Astralbase, Bitmesh, and Thermograph commits.
The ranker-input audit contains no verifier-derived fields.
Claims identify the target as a bounded board-form identity and keep model-guided orthodox-chess discovery unproven.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_68_discovery_contract_and_anchor_replay.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 68 graph, roles, and dependency order validate.
Typed identities, canonical bytes, cross-object invariants, and ranker isolation pass.
The bounded target replays inside a complete four-candidate discovery bundle.
The evidence and claim documents preserve the board-form and orthodox-chess boundary.

integration_gates.command: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v && python3 engine/test.py && python3 scripts/validate_waves.py && git diff --check
